macro_segments.title: The Definition and Mechanics of Sending Away the Mother Bird (Shiluach Ha-Em)

micro_segments.title: How far must the mother bird be sent away according to Rav Yehuda
How the bird is sent away: Walking versus Flying according to Rav Huna and Rav Yehuda
The case of one who clipped the mother bird's wings and released it, with Rav Yehuda's response

macro_segments.title: The Status of Shiluach Ha-Em as a Mitzvah: Before or After Transgression

micro_segments.title: Discussion of whether giving rabbinic lashes is appropriate given the mitzvah of shiluach can fix the transgression
Whether the mitzvah of shiluach applies from the outset or only after transgression, with analysis of Rashi and Rambam

macro_segments.title: Single Egg or Chick and Rava's Question to the Inquirer

micro_segments.title: The inquirer's question about whether the mitzvah applies to a single egg or chick
Rava's ruling that even one egg or one chick requires shiluach, and his laying a net for the bird
Discussion of potential suspicion regarding Rava's pesak and the use of his left hand

macro_segments.title: Wild Doves in the Dovecote: Ownership and the Obligation of Shiluach

micro_segments.title: Doves of the dovecote and attic as birds not under control and the issue of stealing (gezel)
When the egg emerges: acquisition by one's field and the obligation of shiluach
The prohibition on taking possession of eggs while the mother is hovering

macro_segments.title: Technical Workarounds: Transferring Ownership of Chicks via Banging the Dovecote

micro_segments.title: Levi bar Shimon's transfer of the fruit of the dovecote to Rabbi Yehuda
Shmuel's advice to bang the dovecote to cause mothers to lift and enable possession
Discussion of why hagbaha (lifting) works for acquisition rather than kinyan sudar

macro_segments.title: The Prohibition Against Taking the Mother with the Chicks and Final Mishnah

micro_segments.title: The absolute prohibition on taking the mother bird with the chicks, even for a tzaraat purification

macro_segments.title: The Reward of Shiluach Ha-Em: This World versus the World to Come

micro_segments.title: The difficulty of the promise of long days given the small cost of the mitzvah
The case of the child who fell from the tower while performing shiluach and kibbud av va-em
Resolution that rewards of Torah must refer to the world to come, not this world
Protection of those engaged in mitzvah, and the exception when danger is prevalent
Elisha ben Avuyah's loss of faith due to seeing a person die while performing shiluach

topical_tags.term: Lo Tikach Ha-Em Al Ha-Banim (Do not take mother with chicks)
Mitzvah reward in this world versus world to come
Shliach Mitzvah (One performing a mitzvah is protected)
